_note: Heritage page. Biggest content blocker per CONTENT-INVENTORY: real family names + portraits. Family entries shipped as null placeholders so DraftField components render in preview until client provides.
lede: Terry's Place opened in 1980 with a simple promise: cook everything to order, never skimp on portions, and treat every customer like a regular. Forty-five years later, we're still doing it the same way — now run by Terry's son and grandson.
story:
Terry's opened on NE 36th Avenue in 1980 with a simple promise: a fair price, a big plate, and a place where neighbors run into neighbors. Three generations later, that's still the whole job.

You'll see the same faces behind the bar that you saw last Sunday — and probably last Sunday a year ago.
pullQuote: If a guy can't eat it all, you cooked it right.

storyRows.title: Terry Built the Bar.
body: Terry Sr. opened the doors on NE 36th Ave in 1980 with a Frigidaire, a fryer, and a hand-painted sign. He'd worked the line at three Ocala restaurants before deciding to do it his way: half-pound burgers, hand-breaded wings, and never, ever a microwave.
The dining room sat 24. The bar sat 8. The first month, we sold 11 burgers a day. The second month, we sold 80.
storyRows.pullQuote: If a guy can't eat it all, you cooked it right.

storyRows.title: His Son Runs the Kitchen.
body: Terry Jr. took over in 2003. Same recipes, same portions, same rule about pre-cooking nothing. He added the wing menu (now 11 sauces), expanded the bar, and put 22 TVs on the walls so nobody misses a Gators game.
His son — Terry III, but everybody calls him "Tre" — handles the front of house and runs the events. Three generations, one room, no shortcuts.

timeline.what: Doors open on NE 36th Avenue. Round one.
Third generation steps in. Same kitchen, same recipes.

groupPhoto.alt: Terry's Place staff in front of the brushed-metal Terry's wordmark

_pendingFromClient: real family names for the three generations
